zone_6b_notes: Plant 1-year-old crowns 6–8 inches deep in spring as soon as soil can be worked — early April in Farmington. Do not harvest the first year; harvest lightly the second year; full harvest from year 3 onward. Asparagus is a 15–20 year perennial investment — site selection is permanent. Rich, well-draining soil is essential; Missouri clay must be heavily amended. Jersey Knight is the standard reliable all-male hybrid (higher yield, no seedlings). After harvest ends in June, let ferns grow full height to feed the crowns for next year. Cut ferns to ground in late fall.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant crowns early April — permanent bed

water: 1–2 inches/week during establishment; drought tolerant once established
